content:
# Multi Variable Regression

**Summary**: A statistical technique that uses several explanatory variables to predict the response of a dependent variable.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1994-muhammad|Muhammad, 1994]] employed multi-variable-regression to develop empirical equations relating dimensionless groups to gelation time.

- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] used multi-variable-regression to develop a new correlation for choke size based on oil flow rate, tubing pressure, and mixture density.

- [[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]] applied multi-variable-regression to develop traditional GMPM models.

- [[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001]] used multi-100-linear-regression to develop a new dewpoint pressure correlation for gas-condensate systems.

## Instruments and Software



- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] used multi-variable-regression to develop a new correlation for choke size based on oil flow rate, tubing pressure, and mixture density.

- [[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]] applied multi-variable-regression to develop traditional GMPM models.

- [[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001]] used multi-100-linear-regression to develop a new dewpoint pressure correlation for gas-condensate systems.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used to relate the gelation time to the most affecting parameters via derived dimensionless groups. ([[1994-muhammad|Muhammad, 1994, p. 24]])

- The T-statistics for all coefficients were significantly different from zero at the 99 percent confidence level. ([[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992, p. 85]])

- The CatBoost model showed lower bias at both high and low PGA levels compared to other models. ([[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025, p. 70]])

- The model developed using multiple linear regression achieved an R-squared of 0.897 and a correlation coefficient of 0.9471. ([[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001, p. 72]])
